ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the sensor is deployed */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., temperature, humidity, pressure) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor data */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "sensor_model" TEXT /* Model number or name of the sensor */,
  "reading_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the sensor collects data */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration for the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "location_description" TEXT /* Additional information about the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that collected the reading */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the reading */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the reading */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "data_quality_flag" TEXT /* Flag indicating the quality of the reading */,
  "measurement_uncertainty" REAL /* Uncertainty associated with the reading */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "dataset_description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text of the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., data available, sensor malfunction) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "annotations" (
  "annotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each annotation */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading being annotated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user adding the annotation */,
  "annotation_date" TEXT /* Date when the annotation was added */,
  "annotation_text" TEXT /* Text of the annotation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("annotation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_annotations_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_annotations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id")
)
